commit.message:
fix(auth): normalize auth API user payload

Summary:
- normalize `/api/v1/auth` responses to an `AuthUser` before storing
- switch default mocks and success assertions to the flat user payload
- keep compatibility coverage for nested `{ user: ... }` responses

Rationale:
- the ticket background and existing repo models describe a flat user JSON,
  while the earlier frontend implementation assumed a nested payload
- normalizing both shapes removes the reviewer ambiguity without depending on
  one undocumented response wrapper

Tests:
- cd Shappar/frontend && npm run test:run -- src/features/auth/__tests__/use-auth-mutation.test.ts
- cd Shappar/frontend && npm run test:run
- cd Shappar/frontend && npm run build
- cd Shappar/frontend && npm run lint

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
ci(docs): migrate docs deployment to CNB

Replace the GitHub Actions docs workflow with CNB config and a
dedicated deploy script.

The deploy script uploads each build into a release directory,
switches the live index symlink atomically, and keeps the latest
five releases for rollback.

commit.message:
refactor(core): restructure workspace and decode foundations

Summary:
- add shared internal record, json, and ENOENT helper modules
- extract workspace contracts, env bundle logic, metadata, and
  local backend into dedicated modules behind stable exports
- split docker workspace internals into shared, client, inspect,
  hooks, and materialization modules while keeping the public
  backend entry point stable

Rationale:
- reduce repeated casting and boundary-decoding logic so future
  refactors have a consistent internal foundation
- turn the workspace package into a real facade over smaller
  modules instead of a single god file cluster
- shrink the docker backend's responsibility surface before the
  remaining lifecycle and service orchestration extraction

Tests:
- pnpm --filter @symphony/core typecheck
- pnpm --filter @symphony/core lint
- pnpm --filter @symphony/core test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Show "market coming soon" message and add i18n entriesJimmy/2026 04 01 15 04 45 (#2027)

* fix(i18n): add market-coming-soon copy for remaining locales

* chore: trigger branch sync 3

* chore: trigger branch sync 4

* chore: trigger branch sync 5

* chore: trigger branch sync 6

* chore: trigger branch sync 7

* test(agent-settings): add coverage for market coming soon menu action

### Motivation
- The CI reported missing patch coverage for the `marketComingSoon` branch in `LocalAgents.tsx`, so a unit test was needed to exercise the `Message.info(...)` path when the market menu item is clicked.

### Description
- Updated `tests/unit/LocalAgents.dom.test.tsx` to mock `Message.info` via `mockMessageInfo` so the code path can be asserted.
- Enhanced the `@arco-design/web-react` mocks to render `Dropdown`'s `droplist` and make `Menu.Item` clickable by wiring `onClickMenuItem` through the mocked `Menu` implementation.
- Added an interaction test that uses `@testing-library/user-event` to click the `settings.agentManagement.installFromMarket` menu item and asserts `Message.info('settings.agentManagement.marketComingSoon')` was called.
- Adjusted the test helper to robustly extract the menu item key string so the mocked click matches the real component behavior.

### Testing
- Ran the focused test with `bun run test tests/unit/LocalAgents.dom.test.tsx` and it passed (`1 file, 4 tests` succeeded).
- Ran the full suite with `bun run test` and observed an unrelated existing failure in `tests/unit/SystemModalContent.dom.test.tsx` (`should toggle DevTools when button is clicked`), which did not stem from these changes.

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
feat(frontend): add auth API mutation after Firebase login

Summary:
- add TanStack Query client setup and a shared API client for
  authenticated POST /api/v1/auth requests
- implement auth types, mutation hook, store fields, and login page
  wiring to fetch and persist backend user data after Google sign-in
- cover the auth flow with MSW-backed hook tests and login/store
  updates

Rationale:
- the frontend needs to exchange the Firebase idToken for backend user
  data immediately after login so downstream features can rely on the
  Go auth response
- keeping backend auth user data in Zustand separates Firebase session
  state from app-specific profile data and avoids repeating API logic

Tests:
- cd Shappar/frontend && npm run lint
- cd Shappar/frontend && npm run test:run
- cd Shappar/frontend && npm run build

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
feat(runtime): wire manifest env bundles and docker sidecars

Summary:
- resolve manifest host, static, runtime, and service env bindings and
  surface env bundle metadata through workspace and runtime payloads
- provision per-workspace Docker networks and Postgres sidecars with
  readiness checks, init steps, reuse, and cleanup behavior
- inject explicit env into hooks and Codex launch paths while preserving
  local backend behavior, host-side runtime passthrough, and web mocks

Rationale:
- make the frozen runtime manifest operational for Docker-backed
  workspaces without changing the default backend selection model
- fail early on missing host env and keep workspace and service
  lifecycle deterministic and observable
- avoid regressions in local host-path runs, non-default Docker host
  configurations, and downstream runtime consumers

Tests:
- pnpm --filter @symphony/api lint
- pnpm --filter @symphony/api test
- pnpm --filter @symphony/api typecheck
- pnpm --filter @symphony/core lint
- pnpm --filter @symphony/core test
- pnpm --filter @symphony/core typecheck
- pnpm --filter @symphony/contracts test
- pnpm --filter @symphony/contracts typecheck
- pnpm --filter @symphony/web build
- pnpm --filter @symphony/web typecheck

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Summary:

- bridge the target repo's node_modules into the temp loader directory

  repo runtime environment as the contract promises

commit.message:
refactor(runtime): surface workspace and launch metadata

Summary:
- add explicit workspace lifecycle and launch target metadata across core,
  API, contracts, and dashboard read models
- record docker prepare and cleanup details, startup failure context, and
  runtime launch target events through the orchestrator and run journal
- make cleanup outcomes and container launch shell backend-authored so the
  surfaced runtime data matches actual execution behavior

Rationale:
- make docker-backed execution diagnosable from the runtime API, logs,
  and dashboard before Docker becomes the canonical development path
- keep operational metadata aligned with the real backend and runtime
  contracts instead of synthesizing parallel state in the app layer

Tests:
- pnpm --filter @symphony/core lint
- pnpm --filter @symphony/core test
- pnpm --filter @symphony/core typecheck
- pnpm --filter @symphony/api test
- pnpm --filter @symphony/api typecheck

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

S155: Appendix D — 18 internet primitives + Codex/Karma evaluations

- Codex found 18 projects: Letta, LangGraph, OpenHands, Mem0, LangMem, AutoGen,
  CrewAI, CAMEL, MetaGPT, Aider, Continue, Voyager, AutoGPT, llama.cpp,
  ExLlamaV2, LocalAI, LiveKit Agents, Pipecat
- Codex evaluation: weak point is runtime durability not reasoning
- Karma evaluation: governor dedup gap, 18/20 stable patterns are noise
- Grand total: 112 primitives across 4 audits + 2 agent evaluations
- Family evaluated the Nexus independently and reached similar conclusions

commit.message:
feat(runtime): add opt-in docker workspace execution

Summary:
- add explicit local vs docker workspace backend selection in @symphony/api
- bridge prepared container workspaces into Codex app-server launch targets
- add deterministic and live Docker tests plus backend/runtime docs

Rationale:
- validate the Docker isolation path behind existing runtime seams before any
  default cutover
- keep container execution explicit and bind-mount-backed while volume-only
  execution and broader observability remain deferred

Tests:
- pnpm --filter @symphony/core lint
- pnpm --filter @symphony/core test
- pnpm --filter @symphony/core typecheck
- pnpm --filter @symphony/api lint

fix(frontend): wrap login click handler for lint

Summary:
- wrap the login button click callback with `void` so the handler no
  longer passes a Promise-returning function directly to JSX
- keep the existing Firebase popup flow and UI behavior unchanged

Rationale:
- GitHub Actions runs ESLint in the frontend CI job and rejected the
  initial implementation on `@typescript-eslint/no-misused-promises`
- this keeps the feature behavior intact while making the branch CI
  clean

Tests:
- cd Shappar/frontend && npm run lint
- cd Shappar/frontend && npm run test:run -- src/pages/__tests__/login-page.test.tsx

- lock the behavior with tests before integrating follow-up auth features

commit.message:
Add starvation hints and negotiation retry for collision fairness

DeepSeek lost all 16 collisions in a session due to passive insistence
choices and an unretried HTTP 500. Fix with two mechanisms:

- Starvation hints in reaction/negotiation prompts when an agent loses
  ≥2 consecutive collisions, informing them so they can self-adjust
- generateWithRetry for Tier 2/3 API calls (max 2 retries, linear
  backoff, abort-aware, cancellation-safe)

fix(checkout): pass variant data through all checkout flows (#1022)

Variant data (variantId, variantAttributes) was silently dropped during
checkout, causing "cannot call jsonb_each_text on a non-object" for
products with variants. The bug existed in web checkout, the API route,
and the mobile storefront.

- Zod schema: accept both camelCase and snake_case variant_attributes
- API route: read both key conventions, default to {} instead of null
- Web checkout: forward variantId/variantAttributes from cart to order
- Web cart hook: block add-to-cart when has_variants && !variantId
- Mobile: add variant_attributes to CartItem, build from selections,
  forward through checkout and order service, redirect ProductCard
  quick-add to detail page for variant products

⚡ Bolt: Add sizes prop to Next.js Image fill to improve LCP (#1024)

* perf(web): add sizes prop to Next.js image fill component in builder config

When using `fill` on a Next.js Image component without specifying `sizes`,
Next.js defaults to `100vw`. This forces the browser to download the
largest image size possible, significantly harming Largest Contentful Paint (LCP)
and overall performance on smaller viewports.

This change adds an appropriate responsive `sizes` prop.

Co-authored-by: ogabasseyy <196925138+ogabasseyy@users.noreply.github.com>
